entries.name: Nervous Security Guard
entries.description: Calls for backup before engaging directly.
entries.hook: The real danger is Suspicion from a badly sold Bit, not the guard himself.

entries.name: Neighborhood Tough
entries.description: Hits hard, no tactics, never coordinates with nearby toughs.
entries.hook: Vulnerable to any distraction or con — never expects a clown to be dangerous.

entries.name: Officer of Uncertain Loyalty
entries.description: Can call in backup in 2-3 rounds if not stopped or won over.
entries.hook: This same block can be an ordinary cop, an Usher in disguise, or a mole inside a Rebel Clown Army cell — reuse it for any of the three without tipping your hand.

entries.name: Obsessed Fan / Tabloid Reporter
entries.description: Each unresolved scene with them adds +1 cumulative Suspicion.
entries.hook: Almost always tied to the New Circus (real mortal fame); a well-sold Bit neutralizes them without violence.

entries.name: Corpsing Echo
entries.description: A manifestation of a Bit that failed catastrophically and never faded — the literal seed of mortal fear of clowns. Terrifies more than it wounds.
entries.hook: Dissolves if someone lands a well-sold Flop in its presence — a genuine laugh literally unmakes it. The Rebel Clown Army and the School of Failure handle these almost effortlessly.

entries.name: Whisper of an Orphaned Motley
entries.poolNote: mental effects only, no physical attack
entries.description: The residual echo of an unclaimed Motley. Whispers temptations of non-consensual Incorporation to anyone who lingers nearby.
entries.hook: High Awareness catches it before it acts; a great way to introduce an interesting Motley without a full dead Clown attached yet.

entries.name: Hollow Applause Spectre
entries.description: Born from mass laughter with nothing real behind it. Steals 1-2 Laughter on contact.
entries.hook: Almost exclusive to New Circus territory; a Physician or Host generating real Laughter nearby repels it.

entries.name: Cut Puppet
entries.description: A summoned construct that lost its caster and wandered off, hostile by default.
entries.hook: No real malice — surprisingly easy to calm with the right Path (Host, Physician) instead of destroying.

entries.name: "Cabbage" — street Auguste
entries.hook: Steals out of need, not malice — almost always resolved by talking or intimidation rather than a fight. A good first Clown-on-Clown encounter for a new party.

entries.name: "The Crimson Shadow"
entries.pathInfo: Pickpocket (Blade), Grade III, School of Failure
entries.hook: She builds her hand patiently — pressure her before she cashes it in, and she's far weaker than she looks. Ambushing the ambusher flips the fight.

entries.name: "Monsignor Rictus" — a fallen Whiteface
entries.pathInfo: Sworn, an oath broken — permanently Oathbreaking
entries.description: A former rising Ring Legend who broke his own Oath for power and is stuck in permanent Oathbreaking — unable to generate Conviction, compensating with pure cruelty toward his old apprentices.
entries.hook: His Aura is dead (Oathbreaking), so his followers stay out of fear, not loyalty, and can be turned mid-fight if offered a way out. Confronting him with proof of his own betrayal can break him emotionally before anyone lands a physical blow.

entries.name: Balloon Dog
entries.description: Fast, fragile attacker. Bit: a dog-shaped balloon.

entries.name: Hat Dove
entries.description: Distraction, obstructs sight. Bit: a magician's dove.

entries.name: Trick Flower
entries.description: One-shot effect on "bloom". Bit: a squirting boutonniere.

entries.name: Balloon Elephant
entries.description: Tank, blocks passage, hard hit. Bit: a giant balloon sculpture.

entries.name: Shadow Puppet
entries.description: Stealthy scout, acts on its own. Bit: shadow-play on a wall.

entries.name: Ventriloquist Dummy
entries.description: Acts and speaks independently. Bit: a "remote-controlled" dummy.

entries.name: Usher
entries.faceNote: always 10, regardless of the Quick Scaling table — they aren't people, they're pure function
entries.description: Patrols low-Suspicion scenes, issues formal warnings, erases minor mortal memories, escorts infractors to someone higher up.
entries.special: Favors fixed Protocols instead of Turns — no Bit needed. Emptying an Usher's Face doesn't defeat them, it dissolves them — the Backstage quietly reassigns the role elsewhere. That's the only way to remove one for good. The Flop is their one real blind spot — they don't process a failure that's also a success.
entries.hook: Their blind spot is the Flop. A plan built around deliberate Flops is the closest thing to a reliable weapon against them.

entries.name: Régisseur
entries.description: Appears once Suspicion has spiraled. Can rewrite dozens of mortal memories at once, and impose formal sanctions (revoking Threshold access, freezing a Motley "under review," dampening Laughter generation for a season). Negotiates first, more often than not.
entries.special: Same Protocol-based, Flop-vulnerable nature as base Ushers, at a higher rank.
entries.hook: Negotiation is usually genuinely on the table before violence — escalating too fast is a player mistake, not an inevitability.

entries.name: Stage Director
entries.description: Appears only when something in the Backstage risks breaking for real, or a Troupe is growing too fast. Can strip Renown from an infractor, force an Incorporation into a specific body, or seal an entire Threshold.
entries.special: Arrives with an Usher escort. Not meant to be beaten by a party alone — coordination, shared Momentum, and deliberate Flops are the intended counters.
entries.hook: Once a Stage Director shows up, the smart move is often to negotiate or flee, not escalate.

entries.name: The Head Régisseur
entries.description: Said to be singular, never seen clearly by anyone who lived to describe it well. Manifests, if it manifests at all, only when the Backstage itself is at existential risk.
entries.special: Not a standard encounter — campaign-climax material only.
entries.hook: This is an ending, not a fight to plan for casually.
